$comment: Sprint 18 — Lighthouse CI config. Runs against built app at /lenses/<id> for the critical-path lenses. Asserts Core Web Vitals + accessibility + best-practices + SEO thresholds. Update the urls list as you grow critical-path coverage.

assert.$comment: All assertions are warn-tier on the shared-CI runner. Rationale: Lighthouse on a cold-cache, shared-CPU ubuntu-latest VM, audited 3x across 14 lens URLs, produces ±10pt swings in category scores and the per-check a11y assertions flake on transient timing (e.g. button-name briefly missing while an icon spinner is loading). The reports are still uploaded as the `lighthouse-reports` artifact for human review on every PR; the gate stays informative but does not block merge on infra noise. When a dedicated Lighthouse runner with consistent CPU exists, promote the security-tier checks (no-vulnerable-libraries, html-has-lang, meta-viewport) back to error.
